The license arrived late, so we could not start the migration.
We are two weeks late because the approval took nine days.
We underestimated the tests. That is why the date moved.
We have a plan: we are going to test in parallel.
Sophia, I want to give you a heads-up before the client call. The migration is delayed.
How bad is it?
Two weeks. The root cause is the license approval. It arrived nine days late.
Nine days? And we are hearing this now?
You are right, and I apologize. I made a mistake: I did not tell you last Tuesday.
OK. What is the impact on the client?
The go-live moves from June 20th to July 5th. Nothing else changes.
And your recovery plan?
We are going to test in parallel and add one weekend run. If it works, we save three days.
I want to give you a heads-up: the migration is delayed.
The root cause is the license approval.
The impact is two weeks on the go-live.
I apologize for the delay, and here is my plan.
We are going to reschedule the go-live to July 5th.

Diogo, we have a problem. The client called me this morning. They heard that the migration is late, and they heard it from someone else, not from you. That is the worst way to get bad news. Please call them today. Tell them what happened, tell them what it means for their date, and tell them your plan. Do not apologize five times. Apologize once, and move to the plan. And give them a new date that you can actually keep.

Thank you for taking the call. I want to be direct with you. The migration is delayed by two weeks. Here is what happened. We needed a license approval, and it arrived nine days late. That is the root cause. Here is the impact. The go-live moves from June 20th to July 5th. Nothing else in the plan changes. I apologize for the delay, and I also apologize for telling you late. Here is my recovery plan. We are going to test in parallel, and we are going to add one weekend run. If that works, we recover three days. I will send you the new plan by Friday, and I will call you every Monday until we go live.

Diogo gives Sophia a heads-up before the client call. He explains the root cause: the license arrived nine days late. Sophia asks about the impact on the client. Diogo gives the new go-live date. Finally, he presents the recovery plan: parallel tests and a weekend run.
